LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF JANOS LIBOR'S MOTION TO AMEND COMPLAINT. PLAINTIFF JANOS LIBOR'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO AMEND THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS GRANTED. UNDER CALIFORNIA'S POLICIES FAVORING LIBERALITY IN AMENDMENT AND RESOLUTION OF ALL DISPUTED MATTERS BETWEEN THE PARTIES IN THE SAME LAWSUIT, THE AMENDMENT NEED NOT RELATE TO CLAIMS OR DEFENSES ORIGINALLY PLEADED. PG&E HAS NOT ESTABLISHED THAT IT WOULD BE PREJUDICED BY THE AMENDMENT, BECAUSE DISCOVERY HAS NOT BEEN COMPLETED, AND THE CURRENTLY ASSIGNED SEPTEMBER 3, 2019 TRIAL DATE IS NOT IMMINENT. IN ANY EVENT, THERE IS A STRONG LIKELIHOOD THAT THE LITIGATION WILL BE STAYED AS A RESULT OF PG&E'S ANTICIPATED BANKRUPTCY FILING. ANY DISCOVERY DISPUTES OR MOTIONS TO CONTINUE THE TRIAL DATE MAY BE ADDRESSED BY THE APPROPRIATE DEPARTMENT. FINALLY, IT IS NOT A GROUND FOR DENYING THE MOTION THAT CAUSATION CANNOT BE ESTABLISHED AS A MATTER OF LAW; CAUSATION ORDINARILY PRESENTS A FACTUAL QUESTION FOR THE JURY. ORDER IS SIGNED. AFTER THE INITIAL HEARING COURT ADDRESS PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST A FURTHER ORDER COMPELLING DISCOVERY. JUDGE SCHULMAN ADDRESS AND HEARS ARGUMENTS STEMMING FROM AN EARLIER ORDER COMPELLING DISCOVERY BY JUDGE KAHN ON JUANARY 23, 2019. JUDGE SHULMAN STATES THAT HE HAS JUST RECEIVED THE SUBMITTED OPPOSITION PAPERS BY DEFENDANT'S COUNSEL AND IS NOT PREPARED TO SUMMARILY RESOLVE THE ISSUES IN FRONT OF HIM AT THIS TIME. JUDGE SCHULMAN DENIES THE REQUEST FOR A FURTHER ORDER COMPELLING DISCOVERY, WITHOUT PREJUDICE, TO THAT RELIEF BEING SOUGHT. Notice Of Motion And Motion To Amend Complaint
Off Calendar Jan-28-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Lori E. Andrus, Esq., Andrus Anderson LLp,Ttelephone 415-986-1400, appearing for Plaintiffs
Gayle L. Gough, Esq., Gough & Hancock, Telephone 415-848-8918, appearing for Defendnat PG&E;

Ruing Argued - The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF JANOS LIBOR's Motion To Amend Complaint.

Plaintiff Janos Libor's motion for leave to amend the First Amended Complaint is granted. Under California's policies favoring liberality in amendment and resolution of all disputed matters between the parties in the same lawsuit, the amendment need not relate to claims or defenses originally pleaded. PG&E has not established that it would be prejudiced by the amendment, because discovery has not been completed, and the currently assigned September 3, 2019 trial date is not imminent. In any event, there is a strong likelihood that the litigation will be stayed as a result of PG&E's anticipated bankruptcy filing. Any discovery disputes or motions to continue the trial date may be addressed by the appropriate department. Finally, it is not a ground for denying the motion that causation cannot be established as a matter of law; causation ordinarily presents a factual question for the jury. Order is signed.

After the initial hearing Court address Plaintiff's request a further order compelling discovery. Judge Schulman address and hears arguments stemming from an earlier order compelling discovery by Judge Kahn on Juanary 23, 2019. Judge Shulman states that he has just received the submitted opposition papers by Defendant's counsel and is not prepared to summarily resolve the issues in front of him at this time. Judge Schulman denies the request for a further order compelling discovery, without prejudice, to that relief being sought. later in due course either upon entry of an order granting relief from stay Or upon PG&E's eventual re[AO-E] emergence from bankruptcy.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulam; Clerk: W. Trupek; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano CSR#8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com =(302/EPS)

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-12-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT PG&E CORPORATION, PACIFIC GAS & ELECTRIC COMPANY's MOTION TO STRIKE PLAINTIFFS' CLAIMS FOR PUNITIVE DAMAGES IN THE COMPLAINT.

Defendants PG&E Corporation and Pacific Gas & Electric Company's motion to strike the punitive damages allegations in the complaint filed by plaintiffs Segvi Curtis and Janos Libor is granted without leave to amend but without prejudice to plaintiffs' filing a motion for leave to add punitive damages allegations if plaintiffs later obtain information that defendants' employees and defendants acted in a way that supports punitive damages liability against either or both of the defendants. The complaint does not allege facts showing either malice, fraud or oppression by defendants' employees or conduct by defendants that supports imposition of punitive damages liability on a corporate employer. Nor does it appear that, at this stage of the case, there is any basis to believe that plaintiffs will be able to do so in good faith.

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M.Goodman, Not Reported. =(302/HK)
